summary: Review found three P0 governance failures: schema predicate fallback can bypass validation, schema AST ignores operators, and import-only AST rejects legitimate import additions.

findings.title: schema_predicate_correction becomes effectively unvalidated when AST is disabled or Go is unavailable
findings.evidence: _v18_ast_check returns success for TDD_AST_VALIDATOR_DISABLE=1, missing go, or missing validator.go, and the schema_predicate_correction path only checks old/new names before relying on that AST call. There is no regex pure-rename fallback for this new exception type, so a missing Go binary or AST killswitch allows non-rename test edits that pass the generic v1.7 edit_existing_tests checks.
findings.recommendation: For schema_predicate_correction, fail closed when AST is unavailable, or implement an actual regex/token fallback that enforces old-name to new-name only before allowing the exception.

findings.title: schema-predicate-check ignores operators and punctuation, allowing non-rename predicate changes
findings.evidence: extractTokens records only ast.Ident and ast.BasicLit values, and isPureTokenRename compares only that reduced stream. A change like require.True(t, got.OldField > 0) to require.True(t, got.NewField >= 0) has the same identifiers/literals except the declared rename, so the operator change is accepted even though AC3 requires any other change to reject.
findings.recommendation: Compare normalized AST structure or token streams that include operators, delimiters, and selector structure; alternatively reject any line where the raw text is not exactly old text with old_name replaced by new_name plus formatting-only changes.

findings.title: import-block-check rejects legitimate import additions because it validates against old file line ranges
findings.evidence: For files with no imports, added lines are allowed only when l.NewLine <= topRegionEnd, but topOfFileBoundary returns earliestDecl - 1. In a normal diff adding import "testing" between package and the first func, the added line's new line number is the first decl's old line, so it is rejected. The same old-range logic also rejects converting a single-line import into an import block because new block lines extend beyond the old import declaration's end line.
findings.recommendation: Validate the post-edit file shape for import_only diffs, or explicitly allow added import declarations at the insertion point before the first non-import declaration and during single-import to block-import expansion.
